config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan hanya demi alasan kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru hak dasar itulah yang melindungi orang dari penyalahgunaan kuasa. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan membatasi hak secara sangat hati-hati, bukan menjadikannya selalu nomor dua. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah yang mampu menyeimbangkan keduanya tanpa menginjak martabat individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the idea that society should automatically come before the individual, because in practice that can become a blank check for abuse of power. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people emphasize social stability, order, and collective responsibility, since those values are deeply familiar and often genuinely useful. But I also think that when individual rights are treated as secondary by default, ordinary people can lose protection precisely when they need it most. A healthy society should protect the common good, but it should do so by respecting basic rights, not by assuming they are disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal yang seharusnya dijaga justru martabat dan perlindungan dasar tiap orang. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi menurut saya ia harus dibangun lewat hak individu yang kuat, bukan dengan mengorbankannya sebagai aturan default. Kalau hak individu dilemahkan, masyarakat memang bisa tampak tertib di permukaan, tetapi rawan tidak adil bagi orang biasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should have automatic priority over the rights of the individual, even though I understand why that idea feels appealing in a country that values order and collective stability. In China, people are often taught that harmony and the common good matter a lot, and I do think those values are important, but I also know how easily “for the public interest” can be used to excuse overreach. If individual rights are treated as secondary by default, ordinary people become too vulnerable when the state or majority decides their interests do not matter. For me, the right balance is not choosing society over the individual, but making sure society is strong enough to protect people without sacrificing basic rights.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari konteks Indonesia, saya justru melihat frasa “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai terlalu longgar untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang tidak proporsional, sehingga orang biasa yang paling mudah dirugikan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dibangun di atas perlindungan hak dasar, bukan dengan menjadikan hak individu sebagai hal yang bisa dikorbankan kapan saja. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling patuh, melainkan yang mampu menjaga keseimbangan tanpa menghapus martabat individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support making society's interests automatically more important than individual rights, because that principle is too easy to abuse. From my perspective in China, I understand why people value stability, harmony, and collective goals, but I have also seen how broad claims about the public interest can leave ordinary people with too little protection. When rights are treated as optional, the burden usually falls on individuals who have the least power to resist. A strong society should serve people, not ask them to give up basic rights as the default price of order.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
